explanation: This LTL formula states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation) then eventually (within a time window of N_DURATION) the robot should explain the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath) or explain the path it is planning to take (explainPath) or both. N_DURATION is a positive integer.

The LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation), then it should explain why it deviated. The explanation should be detailed (detailed), and this should be true globally.

The LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (`planDeviation`), then it should globally ensure that it provides an explanation for the deviation (`explain`). This applies to the whole mission.

The LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, then it will always be the case that the robot should explain why it deviated and provide justification for its actions, along with a detailed explanation. The template used is a combination of a global operator (G) and an implication, ensuring that the behavior holds true indefinitely.

This LTL formula states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it will always provide an explanation (explain). We use the `G` (Globally) operator to assert that this behavior must always be true and `->` (Implication) is used to express the 'if... then...' relationship.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, it must provide an explanation for its actions either immediately or in the future, for a duration of at least N_DURATION time steps.  It uses the atomic propositions 'planDeviation' (robot deviates from the planned route) and 'explain' (provide an explanation).

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan, then globally, the robot must provide an explanation of the path it is currently taking. The property is enforced in a manner that the explanation must be provided within N_DURATION time steps after the deviation. This ensures that the robot provides a real-time explanation if it deviates from its plan.

The robot should provide an explanation if it deviates from the plan. The LTL formula expresses that whenever a planDeviation is detected, then it should always be the case that the robot explains why the plan deviation occurred or a detailed explanation is provided within the next N_DURATION steps. The template uses the G operator to enforce this requirement globally.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation), then the robot should explain the current path it is taking (explainCurrentPath) or should explain the path it intends to take (explainIntendedPath) for a certain duration (N_DURATION) or forever.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it will always (G) be the case that the robot provides (X) an explanation of its current path (explainCurrentPath). This ensures that if the robot deviates, the system will provide an explanation for that deviation.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan (`planDeviation`), then globally, whenever an action occurs, and that action is not a plan deviation, the robot must explain its path. In other words, whenever the robot is not deviating from the plan, it must always explain its path. N_DURATION is not used in this template.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation), then globally (G) it should provide an explanation (explain) of why the deviation occurred, and the explanation should be detailed (detailed). We set N_DURATION = 5.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) it will always provide an explanation of its current path (explainCurrentPath) or, in the future, provide the explanation.

The LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation), then the robot should provide an explanation (explain) within a fixed time period (N_DURATION).  The "G" (Globally) operator ensures this holds true throughout the mission.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot's actions deviate from the plan (planDeviation), then the robot should explain why it deviated and, moreover, the explanation should encompass alternative courses of action (explain & explainAlternatives).  N_DURATION specifies the time window for the explanation.

This LTL formula states that if the robot deviates from the plan, then globally (always), if an explanation is requested, then the robot should always provide its current location or the mission will be attempted to start or it is currently undertaking.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then globally, the robot must provide an explanation about the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath). The explanation will be provided during the same time-step the deviation occurs.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation), then globally it must explain its current path (explainCurrentPath) for a duration of N_DURATION time steps. The template used is a G (Globally) operator, implying that the explanation must be provided at all times after the deviation.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route, it must explain the current path it is taking. The property uses a "G" (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true throughout the entire execution.  The implication in the LTL uses the atomic proposition planDeviation as a condition, and the atomic proposition explainCurrentPath as the consequence of this condition.  It uses the fact that "planDeviation" is true implies "explainCurrentPath".

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation), then globally, if a developer (developer) is interacting with the robot, then the robot should provide an explanation of the current path it is taking (explainCurrentPath) or the robot provides information about the path it is currently taking (returnCurrentPath) or if the robot is behaving in a way that deviates from the expected or planned behavior (unexpectedBehaviour). The property combines the idea that when there's a plan deviation in the presence of a developer, the system needs to address the deviation (explainCurrentPath | returnCurrentPath | unexpectedBehaviour).
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) | ((X bool_exp3) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) | ((X bool_exp3) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))))

explanation: This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) then the robot must provide a detailed explanation (detailRequested) or for a certain period (N_DURATION) an explanation including why the robot has chosen the route it has.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route, then the robot will explain why the robot is doing something or provide an explanation, or explain the off-normal condition or detail requested. We use template 12 because it captures the "if...then" structure, with the antecedent being a plan deviation and the consequent being a request for explanation or action.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, then it is always the case that if `infoRequest` is true, then the robot should provide a detailed explanation of the current path or explain why the robot is suitable for the mission, either immediately or within a certain time window.

This LTL formula states that if the robot deviates from the plan, which is represented by planDeviation, then it should always explain the current path, captured by explainCurrentPath, or at some point in the future, it will explain the current path, which is represented by explainCurrentPath. The use of the G operator signifies 'globally' or 'always', and the V operator signifies 'release', a weaker form of until.  N_DURATION is the time period for this behaviour. The implication (->) states that planDeviation triggers the requirement for explainCurrentPath.

This LTL formula states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it should always be the case that the robot explains its current path (explainCurrentPath) or provides its current location (returnCurrentLocation) for a fixed duration (N_DURATION). The formula uses a temporal logic operator G (Globally) to ensure this behavior holds throughout the entire mission. The use of the 'or' operator in the formula allows the robot to provide either an explanation of its path or its location, or both, to satisfy the requirement.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it should always explain the reason for the deviation (provideReason) for a duration of at least 1 time step, effectively ensuring immediate justification of the deviation.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the planned path, it should always explain why (provideReason) and should explain the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath).  The template uses 'G' (Globally) to ensure this holds true at all times.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan, then globally (always), if a detailed explanation is not requested, then the robot should eventually provide an explanation of the path it is currently taking or the path it is planning to take. N_DURATION is set to 1, meaning a reasonable amount of time should be given to the robot for returning the explanation.

The LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation), then globally (G) from that point on, if an information request (infoRequest) is made, the robot will always (G) respond with an explanation of its current path (explainCurrentPath) and the path it intends to take (returnIntendedPath) over a period of time (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (`planDeviation`), then it must always explain the path it intends to take (`explainIntendedPath`). The property uses a 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this condition holds at all times.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it should always provide an explanation for the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath) or for the path it is intending to take(explainIntendedPath) within a fixed duration. This captures the requirement for explanation upon deviation.

This LTL formula states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan, then eventually, within a certain duration, the robot should explain the path it is planning to take or the robot is currently explaining the path it is planning to take. Specifically, the formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to assert a condition that holds true throughout the mission. It also uses the '->' (Implication) to connect 'planDeviation' to 'explainPath'. The '[0,N_DURATION]' is used to limit the time.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, it should always explain why, within a fixed time window (N_DURATION). Specifically, it uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true at all times. The `bool_exp1` is defined as  `planDeviation`, which is the deviation from the plan,  `bool_exp2` is true,  `bool_exp3` is `explain`, and `bool_exp4` is `false`. This structure captures the requirement that any deviation from the plan necessitates an explanation.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route, it must explain why, within a certain time frame (N_DURATION). The property uses the "G (Globally)" operator, which means the condition applies throughout the entire mission.

The LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation), then it should eventually explain why (explain). The explanation must also be detailed (detailRequested). N_DURATION is set to 5.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, it must always provide an explanation of its current path and location. The use of the G operator ensures this holds true throughout the entire execution. The property ensures the robot explains its current location.
This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the planned route (planDeviation) at any point in time, then it should always explain its current path (explainCurrentPath).

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the planned route (`planDeviation`), then globally, the robot must explain the current path it is taking (`explainCurrentPath`) or it must explain the path the robot intends to take (`explainIntendedPath`) for a duration of at least 1 time step. The OR condition ensures that if one is true, the whole property is satisfied.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation) then globally, for a duration, it must provide an explanation for the deviation (explain). The template ensures that the explanation is given and persists for a specified period.

The LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route (planDeviation), then globally, when the robot is not changing its plan (no changePlan), the robot must explain its current path and either globally provide a detailed explanation or for a duration provide a detailed explanation.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the planned path (planDeviation) at any point in time, then eventually, within a certain time window (N_DURATION), the robot will explain why the robot is doing something (explain), AND the explanation will be detailed (detailed).

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, it must always provide an explanation for this deviation. This is a safety and transparency requirement ensuring the robot's actions are always accountable.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation) at any point in time, then globally, the robot should explain its current path (explainCurrentPath) within a time window of N_DURATION. This ensures that the robot always provides an explanation of its path when it deviates from the plan, providing transparency.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan (planDeviation), then the robot must explain the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath) or explain why it's deviating by explaining why the robot is doing something (verifyAction) within a certain time frame (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the planned path (planDeviation), then globally, the robot must provide an explanation (explain). N_DURATION is a fixed value, and it specifies the duration for which the condition should hold.

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan, then the robot should always provide an explanation for its path, either immediately or within a fixed duration. It uses the template G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))).

This LTL property states that if the robot deviates from the plan (planDeviation), then it must explain the path it is currently taking (explainCurrentPath) or will explain the path it is currently taking within N_DURATION time steps.  It uses the template G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route, then the robot should provide an explanation of why this deviation has occurred, for a duration of 5 time steps. This ensures that any unexpected behavior is immediately explained, and the rationale is provided within a reasonable timeframe.

This LTL formula states that if the robot's actions deviate from the plan, then it should explain why it deviated and what it is doing now (explainCurrentPath) for a certain duration. The formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true throughout the mission.

This LTL property states that if the robot's actions deviate from the planned route, it should, at all times in the future, provide a detailed explanation. The explanation should include the robot's current location.  This property leverages template 177, which uses the global operator (G) to ensure that the condition holds true from the current state onwards.  The antecedent of the implication (bool_exp1) specifies planDeviation which is the condition.  The consequent (bool_exp3) combines two atomic propositions, explain and returnCurrentLocation, to make sure that when the robot deviates, it explains the details and its location.  N_DURATION is not applicable in this case since the global operator takes care of the "always" condition.
